problems.title: Two Sum
problems.description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target. You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: list of integers (nums)
Line 2: integer (target)
problems.outputFormat: List of two integers [i, j]
problems.constraints:
2 <= nums.length <= 10^4
-10^9 <= nums[i] <= 10^9
Exactly one valid answer exists

problems.title: Valid Parentheses
problems.description: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid. An input string is valid if open brackets are closed by the same type of brackets and in the correct order.
problems.outputFormat: true or false
problems.constraints:
1 <= s.length <= 10^4
s consists of parentheses only '()[]{}'

examples.explanation: The parentheses match correctly.
Different types of brackets cannot close each other.

problems.title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
problems.description: You are given the heads of two sorted linked lists list1 and list2. Merge the two lists into one sorted list. The list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists. Return the head of the merged linked list.
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: list1 elements (space-separated integers)
Line 2: list2 elements (space-separated integers)
problems.outputFormat: Merged sorted list (space-separated integers)
problems.constraints:
The number of nodes in both lists is in the range [0, 50]
-100 <= Node.val <= 100
Both list1 and list2 are sorted in non-decreasing order

examples.explanation: Merging both sorted lists results in a single sorted list.
Both lists are empty.

problems.title: Climbing Stairs
problems.description: You are climbing a staircase. It takes n steps to reach the top. Each time you can either climb 1 step or 2 steps. In how many distinct ways can you climb to the top?
problems.outputFormat: Integer representing number of distinct ways

examples.explanation: There are two ways: (1+1) and (2).

problems.title: Same Tree
problems.description: Given the roots of two binary trees p and q, write a function to check if they are the same or not. Two binary trees are considered the same if they are structurally identical and the nodes have the same value.
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: level-order traversal of tree p (use 'null' for missing nodes)
Line 2: level-order traversal of tree q (use 'null' for missing nodes)
problems.outputFormat: true or false
problems.constraints:
The number of nodes in both trees is in the range [0, 100]
-10^4 <= Node.val <= 10^4

examples.explanation: Both trees are structurally identical with the same node values.
The structures of the trees differ.

testCases.input:
1 null 2 null 3 null 4
1 null 2 null 3 null 4

problems.title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
problems.description: Given the root of a binary tree, return its maximum depth. The maximum depth is the number of nodes along the longest path from the root node down to the farthest leaf node.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: level-order traversal of the binary tree (use 'null' for missing nodes)
problems.outputFormat: Integer representing the maximum depth
problems.constraints:
The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 10^4]
-100 <= Node.val <= 100

examples.explanation: The longest path is 3 -> 20 -> 15 or 3 -> 20 -> 7.
The longest path is 1 -> 2.

testCases.input: 10 5 15 3 7 12 18 1 null null null null null null 20
1 2 null 3 null 4 null 5 null 6 null 7 null 8 null 9

problems.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
problems.description: You are given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a given stock on the ith day. You want to maximize your profit by choosing a single day to buy one stock and choosing a different day in the future to sell that stock. Return the maximum profit you can achieve from this transaction. If you cannot achieve any profit, return 0.
problems.outputFormat: Integer representing maximum profit
problems.constraints:
1 <= prices.length <= 10^5
0 <= prices[i] <= 10^4

examples.explanation: Buy on day 2 (price=1) and sell on day 5 (price=6), profit = 5.
Prices keep decreasing so no profit is possible.

problems.title: Valid Palindrome
problems.description: Given a string s, determine if it is a palindrome, considering only alphanumeric characters and ignoring cases.
problems.outputFormat: true or false
problems.constraints:
1 <= s.length <= 2 * 10^5
s consists only of printable ASCII characters

examples.input: s = "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"
examples.explanation: After removing non-alphanumeric characters and ignoring case, it becomes 'amanaplanacanalpanama'.
After processing, it becomes 'raceacar' which is not a palindrome.

testCases.input: A man, a plan, a canal: Panama

problems.title: Linked List Cycle
problems.description: Given head, the head of a linked list, determine if the linked list has a cycle in it. There is a cycle in a linked list if there is some node in the list that can be reached again by continuously following the next pointer.
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: list elements (space-separated integers)
Line 2: position pos where tail connects (−1 if no cycle)
problems.outputFormat: true or false
problems.constraints:
The number of nodes in the list is in the range [0, 10^4]
-10^5 <= Node.val <= 10^5
pos is -1 or a valid index in the linked list

examples.explanation: The tail connects to node index 1 creating a cycle.
There is no cycle in the list.

problems.title: Reverse Bits
problems.description: Reverse bits of a given 32 bits unsigned integer.
problems.outputFormat: Unsigned integer representing the reversed bit value
problems.constraints: The input must be a binary string of length 32

examples.explanation: Reversing the bits results in 00111001011110000010100101000000.

problems.title: Number of 1 Bits
problems.description: Write a function that takes an unsigned integer and returns the number of '1' bits it has (also known as the Hamming weight).
problems.outputFormat: Integer representing the number of set bits
problems.constraints: The input must be a binary string of length 32

examples.explanation: The input binary string has three '1' bits.
The binary representation contains a single '1'.

problems.title: Reverse Linked List
problems.description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list, and return the reversed list.
problems.outputFormat: Reversed linked list (space-separated integers)
problems.constraints:
The number of nodes in the list is in the range [0, 5000]
-5000 <= Node.val <= 5000

examples.explanation: The linked list is reversed.
The list is reversed.

problems.title: Contains Duplicate
problems.description: Given an integer array nums, return true if any value appears at least twice in the array, and return false if every element is distinct.
problems.outputFormat: true or false

examples.explanation: The number 1 appears twice.
All elements are distinct.

problems.title: Invert Binary Tree
problems.description: Given the root of a binary tree, invert the tree, and return its root.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: level-order traversal of the binary tree (use 'null' for missing nodes)
problems.outputFormat: Level-order traversal of the inverted binary tree
problems.constraints:
The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 100]
-100 <= Node.val <= 100

examples.explanation: Each node's left and right children are swapped.
Left and right children are inverted.

problems.title: Valid Anagram
problems.description: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s, and false otherwise.
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: string s
Line 2: string t
problems.outputFormat: true or false
problems.constraints:
1 <= s.length, t.length <= 5 * 10^4
s and t consist of lowercase English letters

examples.explanation: Both strings contain the same characters with the same frequencies.
The characters differ.

problems.title: Meeting Rooms
problems.description: Given an array of meeting time intervals intervals where intervals[i] = [start_i, end_i], determine if a person could attend all meetings.
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: number of intervals n
Next n lines: start end
problems.outputFormat: true or false
problems.constraints:
0 <= intervals.length <= 10^4
0 <= start_i < end_i <= 10^6

examples.explanation: The meeting [0,30] overlaps with the others.
No meetings overlap.

problems.title: Missing Number
problems.description: Given an array nums containing n distinct numbers in the range [0, n], return the only number in the range that is missing from the array.
problems.outputFormat: Integer representing the missing number
problems.constraints:
n == nums.length
1 <= n <= 10^4
0 <= nums[i] <= n
All numbers of nums are unique

examples.explanation: 2 is the only number missing in the range [0,3].
2 is missing from the range [0,2].

problems.title: Counting Bits
problems.description: Given an integer n, return an array ans of length n + 1 such that for each i (0 <= i <= n), ans[i] is the number of 1's in the binary representation of i.
problems.outputFormat: Space-separated integers representing the count of set bits from 0 to n

examples.explanation: Binary counts from 0 to 5.

problems.title: Subtree of Another Tree
problems.description: Given the roots of two binary trees root and subRoot, return true if there is a subtree of root with the same structure and node values of subRoot and false otherwise.
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: level-order traversal of root (use 'null' for missing nodes)
Line 2: level-order traversal of subRoot (use 'null' for missing nodes)
problems.outputFormat: true or false
problems.constraints:
The number of nodes in the root tree is in the range [1, 2000]
The number of nodes in the subRoot tree is in the range [1, 1000]
-10^4 <= Node.val <= 10^4

examples.explanation: The subtree rooted at node 4 matches subRoot.
Node values differ in the subtree.

problems.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
problems.description: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
problems.outputFormat: Integer representing the length of the longest substring without repeating characters
problems.constraints:
0 <= s.length <= 5 * 10^4
s consists of English letters, digits, symbols and spaces

examples.explanation: The answer is 'abc', with length 3.
The answer is 'b', with length 1.

problems.title: Longest Palindromic Substring
problems.description: Given a string s, return the longest palindromic substring in s.
problems.outputFormat: String representing the longest palindromic substring
problems.constraints:
1 <= s.length <= 1000
s consist of only digits and English letters

examples.explanation: Another valid answer is 'aba'.
The longest palindrome substring is 'bb'.

problems.title: Container With Most Water
problems.description: You are given an integer array height of length n. There are n vertical lines drawn such that the two endpoints of the ith line are (i, 0) and (i, height[i]). Find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container such that the container contains the most water.
problems.outputFormat: Integer representing the maximum area of water that can be contained
problems.constraints:
n == height.length
2 <= n <= 10^5
0 <= height[i] <= 10^4

examples.explanation: The maximum area is formed between heights 8 and 7.
The only container possible holds area 1.

problems.title: 3Sum
problems.description: Given an integer array nums, return all the unique triplets [nums[i], nums[j], nums[k]] such that i != j, i != k, j != k and nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] == 0.
problems.outputFormat: List of unique triplets that sum to zero

examples.explanation: Two unique triplets sum to zero.
No three numbers sum to zero.

problems.title: Remove Nth Node From End of List
problems.description: Given the head of a linked list, remove the nth node from the end of the list and return its head.
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: linked list elements (space-separated integers)
Line 2: integer n
problems.outputFormat: Linked list after removing the nth node from the end (space-separated integers)
problems.constraints:
The number of nodes in the list is sz
1 <= sz <= 30
0 <= Node.val <= 100
1 <= n <= sz

examples.explanation: The 2nd node from the end (4) is removed.
The only node is removed.

problems.title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
problems.description: There is an integer array nums sorted in ascending order (with distinct values). Prior to being passed to your function, nums is possibly rotated at an unknown pivot index. Given the array nums after the possible rotation and an integer target, return the index of target if it is in nums, or -1 if it is not in nums.
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: list of integers nums (space-separated)
Line 2: integer target
problems.outputFormat: Integer representing the index of target or -1
problems.constraints:
1 <= nums.length <= 5000
-10^4 <= nums[i] <= 10^4
All values of nums are unique
nums is sorted and rotated
-10^4 <= target <= 10^4

examples.explanation: 0 is found at index 4.
3 is not present in the array.

problems.title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
problems.description: Given two sorted arrays nums1 and nums2 of size m and n respectively, return the median of the two sorted arrays. The overall run time complexity should be O(log (m+n)).
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: space-separated integers (nums1)
Line 2: space-separated integers (nums2)
problems.outputFormat: Float representing the median (one decimal place)
problems.constraints:
nums1.length == m
nums2.length == n
0 <= m, n <= 1000
1 <= m + n <= 2000
-10^6 <= nums1[i], nums2[i] <= 10^6

examples.explanation: Merged array = [1,2,3] and median is 2.
Merged array = [1,2,3,4] and median is (2+3)/2 = 2.5.

problems.title: Trapping Rain Water
problems.description: Given n non-negative integers representing an elevation map where the width of each bar is 1, compute how much water it can trap after raining.
problems.outputFormat: Integer representing total units of water trapped
problems.constraints:
n == height.length
1 <= n <= 2 * 10^4
0 <= height[i] <= 10^5

examples.explanation: The elevation map traps 6 units of rain water.
9 units of water are trapped.

problems.title: Minimum Window Substring
problems.description: Given two strings s and t of lengths m and n respectively, return the minimum window substring of s such that every character in t (including duplicates) is included in the window. If there is no such substring, return the empty string.
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: string s
Line 2: string t
problems.outputFormat: String representing the minimum window substring, or empty string if none exists
problems.constraints:
m == s.length
n == t.length
1 <= m, n <= 10^5
s and t consist of uppercase and lowercase English letters

examples.explanation: The minimum window substring containing A, B, C is BANC.
The entire string is the minimum window.
